channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Politics Friday: Crime, public safety measures drive debates at the Capitol
item.description: Friday at noon, MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst and his guests talk about new public safety bills and criminal justice debates at the Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Senate Republicans approve budget framework, pushing past Democratic objections after all-night vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican senators pushed a $340 billion budget framework to passage early Friday, chugging through an all-night session and Democratic opposition in a step toward unleashing money the Trump administration says it needs for mass deportations and border security that top their agen
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, KEVIN FREKING and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New law clamps down on homeless as blue city advocate admits the 'frustration' is justified
item.description: Fremont, California, voted to ban camping on public property while also creating harsh penalties for anyone who is found 'aiding and abetting' the encampments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Trump's mass layoffs raise the risk of wildfires in the US West, according to fired workers
item.description: The termination letters that ended the careers of thousands of U.S. Forest Service employees mean fewer people and less resources will be available to help prevent and fight wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Martha Bellisle And Claire Rush, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Joint Chiefs chairman heads to US-Mexico border to assess rapid military buildup
item.description: Gen. CQ Brown, chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, is visiting troops along the U.S.-Mexico border to assess the military’s progress in fortifying sections of the wall.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal judge will consider further blocking Trump administration from freezing funds
item.description: A federal judge will consider a request to further block President Donald Trump’s administration from freezing grants and loans potentially totaling trillions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Proposed $500,000 fund would help victims of investment schemes in Nevada
item.description: Victims of investment schemes would be eligible for $25,000 from a fund managed by the Nevada Secretary of State's Office under a proposal presented in Carson City on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Planned medical research grant cuts worry Sarasota scientists
item.description: For more than 20 years, Sarasota-based biomedical research facility The Roskamp Institute has been focused on understanding and finding treatment, prevention, and early detection for Alzheimer's disease and other dementia, along with illnesses facing veterans.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chainsaw-wielding Elon Musk makes ‘Dark MAGA’ appearance with Javier Milei at CPAC
item.description: Wearing a “Dark Gothic MAGA" hat Elon Musk made a surprise appearance at the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) Thursday evening.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration is flouting an order to temporarily lift a freeze on foreign aid, judge says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has kept withholding foreign aid despite a court order and must at least temporarily restore the funding to programs worldwide, a federal judge said Thursday. Judge Amir H. Ali declined a request by nonprofit groups doing business with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Huge cuts in National Institutes of Health research funding go before a federal judge
item.description: A court battle is set to resume over the Trump administration’s drastic cuts in medical research funding.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard And Michael Casey, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge won't immediately block Trump administration’s abrupt halt to Catholic refugee funding
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday refused to immediately block the Trump administration’s abrupt halt to funding of the nation’s largest private refugee resettlement program in a setback to the U.S. Conference of Catholic Bishops.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Mitch Bows Out
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Dems to force votes on Medicaid as it becomes sore point in Senate budget fight
item.description: Democrats are planning to make Republicans take uncomfortable amendment votes, including several on protecting Medicaid from cuts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump loyalist Kash Patel is confirmed as FBI director by the Senate despite deep Democratic doubts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate on Thursday narrowly voted to confirm Kash Patel as director of the FBI, moving to place him atop the nation's premier federal law enforcement agency despite doubts from Democrats about his qualifications and concerns he will do Donald Trump's bidding and go after the Re
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cortez Masto, Rosen introduce proposal to block Trump plan to end birthright citizenship
item.description: Nevada Democratic Sens. Catherine Cortez Masto and Jacky Rosen introduced a proposal Thursday to block President Donald Trump's attempt to end the constitutional right to birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: White House outlines where DOGE savings could go after Trump floats returning 20% to Americans
item.description: The White House was asked about possible DOGE dividend checks for Americans after Trump said taxpayers could receive payouts from DOGE savings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DEI-charged claims fly over contract talks for military school’s leader as lawmaker demands ethics probe
item.description: Rep. Ben Cline said comments from Del. Jennifer Carroll-Foy of Prince William to a VMI boardmember were alarming, after conjecture about VMI's superintendent's contract talks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate confirms Kash Patel to lead FBI
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Kash Patel to serve as the head of the FBI, approving a pick with a string of controversial statements who has been accused by Democrats of directing a purge of bureau employees. Patel was confirmed in a 51-49 vote. Two Republicans, Sens.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: NY Gov Hochul decides NYC Mayor Adams' fate; ramps up oversight of city's top official
item.description: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul will not remove New York City Mayor Adams from office and announced new state and city bills that would add oversight to the mayor’s office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New York governor won't remove NYC mayor, for now, but plans to increase oversight of City Hall
item.description: Gov. Kathy Hochul will not immediately remove New York City Mayor Eric Adams from office, but will instead push for increased oversight of City Hall as he faces intense scrutiny over his relationship with the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Izaguirre And Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: American fans lightly boo 'O Canada' before 4 Nations Face-Off title game
item.description: American fans lightly booed the Canadian national anthem and roared out their own Thursday night before the championship game of the 4 Nations Face-Off, a matchup between the United States and Canada that has taken on collateral importance in the wake of cross-border political animosity.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What is Humphrey's Executor? A look at the 90-year-old Supreme Court decision Trump is targeting
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A month into President Donald Trump 's second term, lawyers for the Republican administration seem intent on provoking a legal fight to overturn a 90-year-old Supreme Court decision known as Humphrey’s Executor that has been critical to the development of the modern U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mitch McConnell won't seek reelection in 2026
item.description: McConnell, the longest-serving Senate party leader in history, chose his birthday to share his decision not to run for another term.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department official urges quick dismissal of case against NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In an extraordinary courtroom showdown, a federal judge pressed the Justice Department on Wednesday on its demand to throw out New York City Mayor Eric Adams’ corruption case, grilling a top official and putting the Democratic mayor under oath to answer concerns that the Trump admini
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump's plan to end taxes on Social Security benefits would be costly
item.description: Donald Trump wants to end federal income taxes on Social Security benefits but that would come at a steep cost.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: State Department designates 5 drug cartels as terrorist organizations
item.description: Tren de Aragua, MS-13 and the Sinaloa and Jalisco cartels are among those named terrorists by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: National Science Foundation fires roughly 10% of its workforce
item.description: NSF fired 168 employees, leaving the agency less equipped to fund a wide range of scientific research.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert, MPR News

item.title: Emotional testimony for 'Jaya's Law' argues for tougher penalties for wrong-way drivers
item.description: A Las Vegas family made an emotional plea to state lawmakers on Tuesday, telling the story of 3-year-old Jaya Brooks, who was killed in a crash involving a wrong-way driver in December 2023.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump signals 25 percent tariffs coming on cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday indicated he would unveil tariffs on imports of automobiles, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals in early April, starting at 25 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former Vice President Kamala Harris to receive NAACP's Chairman's Award
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris will receive the NAACP's Chairman's Award during the civil rights organization’s Image Awards ceremony this weekend. The Chairman's Award honors individuals who excel in public service and leverage their unique platforms to ignite and drive meaningful change.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: FAA firings begin weeks after deadly crash near DC
item.description: The Trump administration has begun firing hundreds of employees at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) just weeks after a deadly crash outside of Washington, D.C., put a spotlight on the agency.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5
